Task Name: Build standard React UI components (buttons, modals, tables, token balance display, transaction history list)
Justification: Pure presentational code with no business logic, no private keys, no on-chain calls. Blast radius = zero if wrong.
Critical Analysis: Too permissive? Edge case: component that renders raw hex from localStorage could leak data.
Nuance & Conditions: Must use only our approved component library + Tailwind. Never hard-code addresses or private keys. If component contains any useEffect with window.ethereum, move to YELLOW.

Task Name: Implement responsive mobile wallet connect button with ENS avatar fallback
Justification: Uses standard wagmi hooks; no custom signing.
Critical Analysis: Challenge: mobile deep-linking to wallets can expose phishing vectors if misimplemented.
Nuance & Conditions: Always YELLOW on mainnet projects. GREEN only on internal testnets.

Task Name: Set up Zustand or Jotai store for UI-only state (theme, modals, filters)
Nuance & Conditions: Store must never contain wallet address, signature, or private data (enforced by lint rule).

Task Name: Configure wagmi/viem connectors (MetaMask, WalletConnect, Coinbase)
Justification: Chain configuration and RPC endpoints directly affect user funds. Wrong RPC can enable MITM.
Critical Analysis: Is it too permissive? Junior could add unvetted custom chain.
Nuance & Conditions: RED if adding new custom chain or custom transport logic. Must be senior-approved even for testnet.

Task Name: Build transaction confirmation modal with gas estimation preview and simulation link
Justification: UX directly precedes user signature; misleading text = social engineering risk.
Critical Analysis: Could be GREEN if purely cosmetic – but wording matters.
Nuance & Conditions: RED for any modal that auto-signs or bypasses user confirmation.

Task Name: Build standard REST/GraphQL CRUD endpoints for off-chain data (user profiles, metadata)
Justification: No private keys, no funds. Rate-limiting and auth handled at middleware layer.
Critical Analysis: Edge case: endpoint that returns signed messages.
Nuance & Conditions: RED if endpoint signs messages or handles private keys.

Task Name: Define Prisma/PostgreSQL schemas for non-sensitive tables
Nuance & Conditions: RED if schema includes encrypted private keys or seed phrases.

Task Name: Write subgraph schema (entities, relations) and basic mappings for standard events (Transfer, Approval)
Justification: Indexing bugs can cause incorrect balances shown to users → indirect financial loss.
Critical Analysis: Too restrictive? Simple ERC-20 subgraph is deterministic.
Nuance & Conditions: GREEN only for internal analytics subgraphs with zero TVL impact. RED for any governance or staking subgraph.

Task Name: Optimize subgraph queries with @derivedFrom and indexing hints
Implement webhook listener for on-chain events (using ethers.js listener)
Justification: Can trigger external actions (e.g., airdrop mint).
Critical Analysis: Challenge: if listener signs transactions, becomes RED.
Nuance & Conditions: RED if service ever holds private keys or signs txs.

Task Name: Implement standard ERC-20, ERC-721, or ERC-1155 using OpenZeppelin Contracts v5
Justification: Even “standard” contracts can have supply-cap or minting logic that must be audited.
Critical Analysis: Is it too permissive? OpenZeppelin is battle-tested, but custom extensions are not.
Nuance & Conditions: GREEN only for exact unmodified OpenZeppelin import with no custom logic. Anything else RED.

Task Name: Write staking contract with reward distribution
Justification: Reentrancy, precision loss, reward manipulation = direct fund loss. Blast radius = total TVL.
Nuance & Conditions: Remains RED regardless of TVL. Even $1 test contract must be human-written.

Task Name: Implement governance (Governor + Timelock) with custom voting strategies
Justification: Governance attacks (flash-loan votes, proposal hijacking) have destroyed protocols.

Task Name: Add role-based access (Ownable2Step, AccessControl)
Justification: Critical but deterministic if using OZ.
Critical Analysis: Too permissive if custom roles or renounce logic.
Nuance & Conditions: RED if any custom renounce or transferOwnership override.

Task Name: Any use of block.timestamp, block.number for critical logic OR randomness (even Chainlink VRF) OR cross-chain bridging OR upgradeable proxy init OR assembly
Justification: All known attack vectors. AI hallucinates safe patterns constantly.

Task Name: Write Dockerfile and docker-compose for non-production services
Kubernetes manifests for production (including resource limits, pod security policies)
Justification: Misconfigured RBAC or network policies = cluster compromise.
Nuance & Conditions: RED for any secret injection or ingress rules.

Task Name: Any configuration involving AWS Secrets Manager, Vault, or private-key injection
Set up Prometheus + Grafana dashboards and Loki logging
Standard GitHub Actions workflow (lint, test, build)
Production deployment trigger (including multisig or Gnosis Safe signing)
Rollback procedure script
Write unit tests (Jest/Hardhat) for frontend/backend
Write Foundry/Hardhat fuzz tests or invariant tests for contracts
Nuance & Conditions: RED for any test that includes private keys or live RPCs.

Task Name: Run security tools (Slither, Mythril, Aderyn) and interpret results
Manual security review or audit preparation
Generate NatSpec comments for contracts
Write deployment guide or runbook
API documentation containing sensitive endpoint examples or keys
